item.title: Junior Staff Post
item.content:encoded: Image could not be sourced reliably and is assumed not compliant with CC 3.0. As such, it has since been removed.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I read the first version posted by original author first, then rewrite, and…

Very little seems to have changed, except for a full tonal change. It's still quite bland.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is pretty…. Meh. No vote for me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Wasn't it a humanoid with the appearance not unlike that of a goblin? Implying that there are scientific standards regarding goblins?

But, off topic moment aside, it's a standard Voct rewrite. And by "standard" I mean it's really good. Changed my vote accordingly.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
You meant goblins with the appearance not unlike that of a jester.
One doesn't simply appear like a jester.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Yes. Anything involving goblins with the appearance of a jester.

Good rewrite, +1 from me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
My god, is there anything Voct can't rewrite?

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Not bad.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Good, it's well done and everything, I just don't want to see "it's a god" become standard.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I don't plan to do that much of it.

Then again, I didn't plan to do this one as a 'thing' until I saw the potential for rewriting it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Goddamn it, this better not become a 'thing', it's fun because of how it can be used but it'll get really boring if I see more than 5 of the damn things.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Admit it. You just like

+ show block
– hide block

lobotomizing gods


Anyway, upvote on the new version.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: (Note: Not really yay)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Yay, triple post!

item.title: Re: Staff Post
item.content:encoded:
I am so using this in a tale.

Upvoted.

item.title: Re: Staff Post
item.content:encoded: I think it's much improved. +1.

item.title: Re: Staff Post
item.content:encoded:
DONE.

What think you?

item.title: Re: Staff Post
item.content:encoded: Ah, and permission from Colton just came through. I'll start the rewrite tomorrow morning.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Yeah, you can.

Sorry for being offline, was busy and then my Internet kinda went down a bit.

item.title: Re: Staff Post
item.content:encoded: Voct, if you haven't started a rewrite already, go for it.

item.title: Staff Post
item.content:encoded: It's been up for two days; I think the author isn't going to respond. I don't have authority to grant you a rewrite, so beginning deletion vote at -18 or so, subject to reversal should a mod or admin grant permission. Though I would suggest you just start anew anyway.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Testing has revealed that only one speaker can control the radio at a time, and it must be powered down for it to register a change speakers. The first person to tell it a station becomes the speaker it recognizes, not the first person to speak around it.

I don't think I need to say anymore. -1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I will back this in every way I can if you do this.

Voct: Master of Rewrites

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I don't know how valuable my opinion is, but I like this a lot. Something about the idea of a crippled Greek deity seems appealingly Neil Gaiman-esque.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
You need to place greater emphasis on the fact that it has three anomalous properties.

The first of these anomalous properties is that it picks up all broadcasts. Everywhere. Perhaps it even picks up broadcasts that can't be detected by any other means.

The second is that it responds to spoken requests in natural language. Only for requests to "change from Broadcast X to Broadcast Y", yes, but "Broadcast Y" can be described in very, very unusual terms. And it only takes requests from its owner — and it's able to distinguish its owner's voice from high-quality recordings of its owner's voice. Although we do know how to reset its ownership matrix, so that's helpful.

Third anomaly is the deafness thing you mentioned. Eventually the owner stops being able to hear anything except the message coming from the SCP.

What I'm thinking is… okay, have you read SCP-1455, the Lagging Door? In particular, have you read the backstory given on the talk page?

+ the secret of 1455
- the secret of 1414

I'm thinking that 1414 is in the same basic predicament as 1455. Just as 1455 can be considered equivalent to Janus the God of Doors, so 1414 is clearly equivalent to Hermes, the God of Messengers.

Hermes was an unimaginably powerful entity whose abilities pertained to messages and the transmission of information, and some even MORE powerful entity lobotomized it, brainwashed it real good, and polymorphed it into this sound system.

Hermes will provide you with any message you ask for. Any message. From anywhere on Earth. The limits of its range haven't been determined, but let's say it can pick up broadcasts from other dimensions/timelines. Messages from off-planet… well, we don't want the Foundation to be able to confirm that extraterrestrial life exists. Maybe when we ask it for "soundtrack of alien life", it gives us audio from SCP-163's habitat?

Loyalty is important, so Hermes only obeys its master. And as the God of Messages, it can easily tell the difference between a message from its master and the sound of a message from its master, which is why it doesn't respond to recordings.

The part about deafness, that's the result of Hermes being a passive-aggressive little shit. It's managed to sneak around the behavioral and mental limits imposed by the brainwashing, so as to fuck with its owner's life. What Hermes does is convey the message-of-the-broadcast to its owner as perfectly as possible, see?

And when you turn the volume up past a certain point, that must mean that you want to receive the information in the message even better, right? The louder the radio, the more you want. Obviously.

So Hermes formulates the broadcast such that you become unable to hear anything other than its message. You can't hear anything else, therefore nothing is drowning out the radio! If there's only one audio source in your world, then it's the only thing you listen to!

… so that's my idea for how to save this one. May I do a major rewrite?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This seems like something somebody would actually invent, and nobody would find it odd. I wouldn't be surprised if Richard Branson is working on something like this right now. Basically, I can't really see this as a SCP. -1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I didn't even think about that not being clear enough… Yeah, you basically tell it channels to receive, frequencies or call number or whatever people usually refer to the station as. I'll go edit.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: How does speaking to it effect anything? Do you tell it to change channels? Do you ask for smooth jazz or a baseball broadcast?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I think I felt compelled to have a [DATA EXPUNGED]. It's gone now. Assuming it saved my edit, it shows it gone on my computer at least. I'm really not sure at all why I put that there, considering I really didn't have anything to put there without it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
However, [DATA EXPUNGED]

whyyyyy

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I'm admittedly terrible at tags. I took out radio (I searched it and got results, not sure if that means it's a tag or not) though.

Polishing where? I'm not the best at editing my own stuff and I'll be the first to admit I need to improve on my editing skills. If it's not too much to ask for, that is. I do genuinely want to improve it, I just don't know where exactly to start.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Ehhhh, not my cup of tea, needs some polishing, I don't believe 'radio' is a tag but I could be mistaken (either read through the tag guides or remove them, Mackenzie or another tag savvy member will fill it in.)

For now I'll withhold my vote.
